title: Benchmark with criterion and detect regressions
description: Run criterion benchmarks with black_box, and report the statistical estimate and whether a change is a real regression or noise.
body: Use when validating a performance-sensitive Rust change. Actions: place benches under benches/ using criterion's Criterion::benchmark_group, wrap inputs and outputs in std::hint::black_box so the optimizer cannot fold them away, run `cargo bench`, and read criterion's report (point estimate, confidence interval, and its 'change: improved/regressed/no change' verdict against the saved baseline). Report the ns/iter, the CI, and the verdict. Do not benchmark a debug build or trust a single noisy iteration. This skill exposes capability only and must not run on a busy machine without runtime permission. Validation signals include criterion reporting a statistically significant change outside the noise threshold and a flat baseline on no-op reruns. Risk boundary: without black_box the compiler may optimize the work away yielding fake speedups; flag suspiciously near-zero times rather than reporting them as wins.
